title: Monitoring the Uniface Router
text: Monitoring the Uniface Router The activities that you can perform with the Uniface Router Monitor depend on whether or not you are a privileged user. You must have privileged user status to use the Uniface Router Monitor to stop and start servers, stop the Uniface Router, and perform other privileged operations. For more information, see Acquire Privileged User Status for the Uniface Router Monitor . Before starting the Uniface Router Monitor, ensure that the Router Monitor’s assignment file urmon.asn is located in your installation directory’s adm subdirectory. To use the Uniface Router Monitor: Start the Uniface Router Monitor. Choose Urouter >Monitor from the Windows Start menu. By default, the Connect dialog is displayed, but this behavior can be altered by editing the monitor settings. Connect the Uniface Router Monitor to the Uniface Router . Optionally, define and save the connection settings and other preferences. Choose File >Settings . For more information, see Specify Preferences for the Uniface Router Monitor . Note: You can define TCP and TLS connection options in the assignment file of the Uniface Router Monitor ( urmon.asn ). Monitor the Performance of the Uniface Router If you want to use the Uniface Router Monitor to start and stop Uniface servers and other tasks, ensure you have privileged user status. This enables you to: Change Uniface Router Settings Define USTs Dynamically Start Uniface Servers from the Uniface Router Monitor Stop Uniface Servers and Routers from the Uniface Router Monitor Related Links Uniface Router Monitor The Uniface Router Monitor is a Uniface Windows application that you can use to fine-tune the performance of the Uniface Router while it is running. Uniface Router The Uniface Router is a multi-threaded process (urouter) responsible for interprocess communication in Uniface applications. It listens for requests from Uniface client applications and routes them to the appropriate Uniface Server, and it passes messages between various Uniface applications. Was this topic helpful?
